name: Crock-Style Kosher Dill Pickles
recipeInstructions:
1. Place a bed of grape leaves at the bottom of the crock.
2. Alternate layers of cucumbers, dill, and garlic to fill the crock.
3. Bring water, vinegar, and salt to a boil in a separate pot.
4. Pour the brine over the cucumbers in the crock, ensuring they are completely submerged.
5. Place more grape leaves on top of the pickles.
6. Weigh down the lid with a heavy plate or weight.
7. Allow the pickles to set in a cool place for 3-5 days, or until they reach the desired flavor.
8. Store any leftover pickles in the refrigerator.
